entry.title: Entangled photons from para-positronium decay: Do coincidences from scattered photons imply a Bell state?
entry.summary: Electron and positron can form a meta-stable bound state called positronium that decays via pair annihilation. We show how polarization-dependent Compton scattering can be used to verify that the two annihilation photons in the spin-zero case (para-positronium) are emitted in a maximally entangled Bell state. Our theoretical approach based on two-photon density matrices connects concepts from relativistic quantum electrodynamics and quantum information theory.
